properties.remark: OAK CREEK AT SEDONA ROSE 7 FEET BETWEEN 615 AND 830 AM. NUMEROUS LOW WATER CROSSINGS IN OAK CREEK CANYON WERE FLOODED.
DRY BEAVER CREEK ROSE 6 FT BETWEEN 11 AM AND 2 PM.

AT THE PEAK OF THE FLOODING THERE WAS ABOUT 1 FT OF WATER FLOWING THROUGH THE RV PARK. ONE LARGE TREE AND SEVERAL SMALLER TREES CAME DOWN IN THE FLOODING AND RED MUD WAS DEPOSITED THROUGHOUT THE RV PARK.
OAK CREEK AT PAGE SPRINGS ROSE 9 FT BETWEEN NOON AND 230 PM.
BEAVER CREEK ROSE 6.5 FT FROM 415 PM TO 7 PM.
BOULDER-FALL ON AZ-89A IMPACTED A VEHICLE, DISABLING IT NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES REPORTED.
WET BEAVER CREEK NEAR RIMROCK STREAM GAUGE (WBVA3) SHOWED ABOUT A 7 FT. RISE IN TWO HOURS (FROM 6 PM TO 8 PM).
EVACUATIONS UNDERWAY ON BLACKHAWK LANE IN SEDONA DUE TO WATER INUNDATING ROADWAY. RIVER GAUGE AT OAKA3 IS 11.87 FEET.
WEST CLEAR CREEK NEAR CAMP VERDE STREAM GAUGE (WCLA3) SHOWED A 5.7 FT. RISE IN 45 MINUTES (FROM 11:00 PM TO 11:45 PM).
BEAVER CREEK AT MONTEZUMA CASTLE STREAM GAUGE (BCMA3) SHOWED A 15 FT. RISE IN TWO HOURS (FROM 9:45 PM TO 11:45 PM).
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT OF A FLASH FLOOD AT THE INTERSECTION OF ANCIENT TRAIL AND THE PUMPHOUSE WASH IN KACHINA VILLAGE.
BLACK CANYON CREEK AT MAREN AVE STREAM GAUGE (YBSA3) SHOWED A 2 FT. RISE IN FOUR HOURS (FROM 8:30 PM TO 12:30 AM).
EAST VERDE RIVER NEAR CHILDS STREAM GAUGE (EVDA3) SHOWED A 10 FT. RISE IN SIX HOURS (FROM 7:45 AM TO 1:10 AM).
TONTO CREEK ABOVE GUN CREEK STREAM GAUGE (TNRA3) SHOWED A 4.5 FT. RISE IN FOUR HOURS (FROM 1:45 AM TO 6:00 AM).
AGUA FRIA AT OLD BLACK CANYON HIGHWAY STREAM GAUGE (AFBA3) SHOWED A 2 FT. RISE IN SIX HOURS (FROM 1:30 AM TO 7:20 AM).
TIME AND LOCATION ESTIMATED BY REPORT, VIDEO, AND RADAR.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED HAIL FALLING THAT WAS THE SIZE OF A QUARTER WITH LARGER HAIL STONES INTERMIXED.
ASOS STATION KINW WINSLOW AIRPORT - GUST FRONT FROM DECAYING STORMS.
A PICTURE OF ONE INCH DIAMETER HAIL WAS SHARED ON SOCIAL MEDIA FROM NORTH PAYSON.
BLACK WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING OVER NORTH PRIMROSE CIRCLE.
BLACK WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING ABOUT 10 INCHES DEEP AND SUBERA ROAD.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT AND VIDEO INDICATED FLOODED, IMPASSABLE ROADS IN WHITERIVER FRIDAY AFTERNOON.
DELAYED REPORT OF FLASH FLOODING THAT DAMAGED ROADS AND SOME BUILDINGS IN PINETOP-LAKESIDE. REPORTS OF 3+ INCHES OF RAIN.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL JUST SOUTH OF THE PARKS EXIT ON I-40.
NUMEROUS POWER POLES DOWN.
SOCIAL MEDIA PHOTOS SHOWING GREATER THAN 18 INCHES OF RUNOFF RAIN FLOWING THROUGH GRANDPA WASH AT THE INTERSECTION WITH MIDDLE VERDE RD. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND SOCIAL MEDIA POST.
2 FOOT RISE REPORTED ON THE VERDE RIVER, NEAR CAMP VERDE.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF FLOODED ROADS IN THE TUBA CITY AREA. REPORTS ARE FROM PUBLIC AND LAW ENFORCEMENT.
USGS RIVER GAGE (AFHA3) REPORTED A 2.27 FT. RISE IN WATER LEVEL IN THE AGUA FRIA RIVER FROM 5:15PM MST TO 7:00PM MST.
USGS GAGE ON CIBECUE CREEK (CBQA3) SHOWED A 2.15 FT. RISE IN WATER LEVEL IN 15 MINUTES (1:15AM MST - 1:30AM MST).
THE JACKS CANYON RIVER GAUGE SHOWED A 4.4 FOOT RISE (780 CFS) RISE AT 538 PM.
A RIVER GAUGE ON THE LITTLE COLORADO RIVER NEAR CAMERON MEASURED AT 5 FOOT RISE IN 15 MINUTES (730 TO 745). THIS WAS AN INCREASE OF 2,911 CFS.
THE RIVER GAUGE ON THE LITTLE COLORADO RIVER NEAR CAMERON MEASURED A TOTAL RISE OF 5.7 FEET IN AN HOUR AND 45 MINUTES WITH A TOTAL INCREASE OF 3213 CFS BEFORE STARTING TO DECREASE.
PAYSON POLICE REPORT MULTIPLE TREES AND POWER POLES DOWN IN THE CITY.
AWOS STATION KPAN PAYSON AIRPORT.
A TON OF WIND DAMAGE FROM A THUNDERSTORM AND 0.75 INCHES OF RAIN. DAMAGE WAS MOSTLY DOWNED TREE BRANCHES.
THUNDERSTORM CAUSED LOTS OF WIND DAMAGE AND 1.10 INCHES OF RAIN IN THE PRESCOTT AREA. DAMAGE WAS MOSTLY DOWNED TREE BRANCHES.
THE AGUA FRIA NEAR HUMBOLDT RIVER USGS GAGE (AFHA3) SHOWED A 4.1 FT. RISE IN 30 MINUTES. THE RIVER LEVEL WENT FROM 0.87 FT. AT 815PM MST TO 5.01 FT. AT 845PM MST.
THE BIG BUG CREEK GAUGE AT SR-69 IN MAYER RECORDED A 2.5 FOOT RISE BETWEEN 12:30-1:30 PM TODAY. WATER LEVELS HAVE CRESTED AND ARE NOW FALLING.
1.8 INCHES OF RAIN MEASURED IN BLACK CANYON CITY THIS MORNING. AGUA FRIA RIVER NEAR OLD BLACK CANYON HIGHWAY WAS AT 3 FT AND RISING. WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS SQUAW VALLEY ROAD.
TONTO CREEK ROSE 5.6 FEET - USGS GAUGE.
APPROXIMATELY 20 MOBILE HOMES DAMAGED ONE NO LONGER HABITABLE, 4 HOMES SUFFERED MODERATE DAMAGE. POWER POLES SNAPPED, AREA IS STILL WITHOUT POWER. ESTIMATES CLOSE TO 100 TREES DAMAGED AS WELL.
JEFFERSON RD NEAR MAYER WAS FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE AROUND 11AM THIS MORNING DUE TO HEAVY RAINFALL IN THE AREA.
AGUA FRIA AT BLACK CANYON CITY HAS RISEN TO 6.5 FEET, UP 5.5 FEET FROM 1 FOOT BASE FLOW SINCE 5:30 PM.
STREAM GAUGE AT SUPAI PEAKED AT 17.1 FT, JUST ABOVE FLOOD STAGE. SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTS OF A FLOODED CAMPGROUND WITH PERSONAL GEAR SWEPT AWAY.

STREAM GAUGE (BCMA3) ON BEAVER CREEK NEAR MONTEZUMA CASTLE NATIONAL MONUMENT REPORTED A RISE OF 2.37 FEET IN ONE HOUR. THIS RISE IN THE STREAM HAPPENED BETWEEN 3:45 PM (2.51 FEET) AND 4:45 PM (4.88 FEET) MST ON THE 4TH OF DECEMBER. THE STREAM CONTINUED TO RISE TO OVER 9 FEET.
SUCCESSFUL SWIFT WATER RESCUE OCCURRED ALONG REAY RD DUE TO A FLASH FLOOD IN BEAVER CREEK. THE RESCUE WAS SUCCESSFULLY COMPLETED BY 1845 MST.
